title: thymus bracteosus subsp. granatensis
intro: Thymus bracteosus subsp. granatensis, also known as the Granada thyme, is a subspecies of thyme plant native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiseptic properties, which can help combat infections and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Granada thyme include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and exhibiting antioxidant properties that can protect against cell damage. Granada thyme can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or essential oil, which can be used topically or in aromatherapy to leverage its medicinal properties and health benefits.
